name: Speed Optimization V1
description: Reduce turns, tokens, and wall-clock time without regressing pass rate. Each hypothesis tests a single config delta against current defaults.

hypotheses.name: Reduce LLM timeout from 60s to 30s
hypotheses.rationale: Most completions finish in <10s. A 60s timeout wastes wall-clock on hung calls. 30s is still generous.
hypotheses.result: neutral — 4s faster wall-clock but +5% tokens. No meaningful improvement.

hypotheses.name: Enable compact first turn
hypotheses.rationale: First turn snapshot is largest. Compact mode reduces token count on the most expensive call.
hypotheses.result: rejected — +35% turns, +66% tokens. Agent compensates for missing context with extra exploration.

hypotheses.name: Reduce retries from 3 to 1
hypotheses.rationale: 3 retries × 60s timeout = 3min wasted on a dead API call. 1 retry is enough for transient errors.
hypotheses.result: promoted — -16% turns, -23% tokens, same pass rate. Benchmark profiles already set retries=1; global default stays at 3 for resilience.

hypotheses.name: Enable micro-planning (2 actions/turn)
hypotheses.rationale: Batching 2 actions per turn halves round-trips for sequential navigate-then-extract patterns.
hypotheses.result: promoted — +8pp pass rate, -10% turns, -19% tokens, -16% duration. Default enabled in config.ts.

hypotheses.name: Enable micro-planning (3 actions/turn)
hypotheses.rationale: More aggressive batching. Risk: stale snapshots between actions.
hypotheses.result: candidate — +8pp pass rate (CI spans zero: -17pp to +33pp), -14% turns, -20% tokens. Positive signal but insufficient power to promote. Needs larger case set.

hypotheses.name: Earlier supervisor intervention (3 turns instead of 5)
hypotheses.rationale: Detect stalls 2 turns earlier. Risk: false positives on slow-loading pages.
hypotheses.result: neutral — +14% turns, +37% tokens. Early intervention causes false positives.

hypotheses.name: Enable link scouting
hypotheses.rationale: Scout pre-ranks links before navigation, potentially avoiding dead-end clicks.
hypotheses.result: rejected — +17% turns, +30% tokens. Scout overhead exceeds navigation savings.

hypotheses.name: Block images and media resources
hypotheses.rationale: Image/media loading adds network latency. Blocking may speed up page loads without affecting text extraction.
hypotheses.result: promoted — -12% turns, -20% tokens, -21% duration. Applied to benchmark profiles (webbench, webbench-stealth); global default keeps images enabled for visual tasks.

hypotheses.name: Auto-escalate vision only on verification failures
hypotheses.rationale: Vision adds ~14% cost. Auto-escalation uses screenshots only when text-only verification fails.
hypotheses.result: rejected — same 100% pass rate but +7% turns, +48% tokens. Auto-escalation adds retry overhead that exceeds vision savings.

hypotheses.name: Combined: micro-plan-3 + vision-auto-escalation
hypotheses.rationale: Stack remaining untested optimizations. Tests if aggressive batching + conditional vision work together.
hypotheses.result: rejected — incomplete (5/6 runs). Both components individually negative on tokens (+48% vision-auto, +15% combo partial). No reason to expect combined win.

hypotheses.name: Zero-LLM workflow replay (--replay)
hypotheses.rationale: Per-phase timing instrumentation shows a run is ~97-98% LLM-latency-bound (~10-13s per brain.decide). Replay re-runs a recorded successful trajectory with zero decide calls, self-healing to the live loop on drift. One claude-code measurement: a recorded run at decideLlmCalls=2/30s replayed at decideLlmCalls=0/11s, completed+verified. Shipped opt-in (default off) pending this promotion gate.
hypotheses.expected_impact: On REPEATED tasks: near-elimination of decide calls (the dominant cost). No effect on novel tasks (no candidate → falls through).
hypotheses.risk: Self-heal bounds the downside to a few fast browser actions before handing to the live agent; final goal-verification prevents blind success. Main risk is a stale replay wasting those actions.
hypotheses.result: mechanism VALIDATED on claude-code/sonnet (gpt-5.4 key dead → default-model promotion gate still pending). Warm-store A/B, 5 reps, local-form-multistep (.evolve/warm-replay-ab.mjs): replay drops decideLlmCalls 6→2 — deterministic, [6,6,6,6,6]→[2,2,2,2,2] — i.e. -67%; decide-latency -59% (65.4s→26.9s); wall-time -46% (79s mean[63-97]→43s[37-55], passes the spread test: |Δ36s|>34s worst-case spread). Pass-rate HELD 5/5→5/5. Fall-through verified: local-dashboard case (0/5, a deterministic 'page closed' harness/fixture error unrelated to replay) records no trajectory → replay correctly drops to the live loop, 0/5, no regression. PROMOTE to default only after re-running this same warm A/B on gpt-5.4 (key refresh required).
hypotheses._eval_note: Replay needs a WARM trajectory store — the standard per-run memory isolation would show zero benefit (nothing to replay). Run a warm-then-replay variant: pass 1 records trajectories (memory on, --replay off), pass 2 measures the same cases with --replay on against the same store. Compare decideLlmCalls / totalDecideMs / duration / pass-rate across passes. Promote to default only if pass-rate holds and decide cost drops on the warm pass.

hypotheses.name: Re-run micro-plan-3 with more statistical power
hypotheses.rationale: micro-plan-3 was a positive-signal CANDIDATE (+8pp pass, -14% turns, -20% tokens) but its bootstrap CI spanned zero (-17pp..+33pp) on the small reachable-4 case set. Re-run on a larger case set to resolve promote vs neutral.
hypotheses.expected_impact: Resolve the candidate: either promote (-14% turns at held pass-rate) or reject.
hypotheses.risk: Stale snapshots between 3 batched actions can mis-target on fast-changing pages.
hypotheses._eval_note: Use a larger case set than webbench-reachable4 (e.g. the full reachable suite) for the power the original run lacked.
